heading: Learn by Watching
body:
Follow along with this video to learn about how you can create and use a
numeric and string [faceted navigation](fts-facet-ref) in your query to group results
and retrieve a count of the results in the groups.

*Duration: 11 Minutes*

[https://youtu.be/90icEZ8fzJE](https://youtu.be/90icEZ8fzJE)

This tutorial describes how to create an index with a [facet definition](fts-facet-definition) on string, date, and numeric fields
in the `sample_mflix.movies` collection. It shows how to run a MongoDB Search
query against those fields for results grouped by values for the string
field and by ranges for the date and numeric fields, including the
count for each of those groups. It takes you through the following
steps:

- Set up a MongoDB Search index with facet definition on the `genres`,
  `released`, and `year` fields in the `sample_mflix.movies`
  collection.

- Run a MongoDB Search query against the `released` field in the
  `sample_mflix.movies` collection for results grouped by values for
  the `genres` field and by ranges for the `year` field.

To complete these tutorials, in addition to the prerequisites listed in
the [Prerequisites](fts-design-patterns-prereqs), you must have a MongoDB
instance running MongoDB 7.0 or later.

In this section, you will create a MongoDB Search index on the `genres`,
`year`, and `released` fields in the `sample_mflix.movies`
collection.
